metadata.text: Secured Delivery with Tamper-Evident Packaging Packages are shipped in tamper-evident packaging. If you see a pink line across or 'bag tampered' message showing, your package could potentially be tampered. Learn what to do next. To view in your preferred language, locate the country flag on the top banner and use the drop- down to select the language. About Tamper-Evident Packaging Tamper-evident bags have unique package IDs. We pack your items securely inside these bags. The package ID is an alpha-numeric code that helps you to identify your order. If you suspect tampering, you can reject a package at the time of delivery. We send your unique package ID via email and push notification when your order is out for delivery. You can also view the ID by selecting Track Package in Your Orders. Before You Accept a Package Check for the following before you accept a package: . The package ID matches the one we sent to you. . The security numbering at the edges of the bag is visible

The package may not have been tampered with. The preceding image shows: . A pink line shows possible tampering. The preceding image shows: . "Bag Tampered" shows potential tampering. About Secure seal Secure Seal is a tamper-evident security feature that helps verify if a product is authentic. Each seal contains unique 3D patterns that cannot be copied or duplicated. The seal also includes multiple security features like heat-sensitive markers and tamper-evident cuts. //, : AM Tamper-evident packaging: Identify if your package was tampered - Amazon Customer Service https://www.amazon.in/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=G7E84JZEZ3YFZAAD /

metadata.text: The Ships in Product Packaging (SIPP) Amazon Vendor Enrollment and Contact SOP Purpose and Scope This document serves as the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Vendors to self-test and certify, enroll, request their ASIN portfolio packaging certification status, and contact Amazon with other general questions about Amazons Ship in Product Packaging (SIPP) Program. Testing Methods For Non-Fragile ASINs (in rigid packaging) under 50lbs and Non-Fragile/Non-Granular/Non-Sharp ASINs (in flexible packaging), the full ISTA-6A test protocol is not required. We will continue to accept the full ISTA- Amazon test for any eligible ASIN, however after June ,  we will require only the drop sequence from the ISTA -Amazon.com-SIOC test to be eligible to certify ASINs. This enables Vendors to conduct drop testing of their own products for SIOC and FFP certification. Vendors may conduct self-drop testing of Non-Fragile ASINs that are under 50lbs

metadata.text: To qualify your own lab to complete the ISTA-6A test, you must have all required lab testing equipment, ISTA Lab Certification, and complete APASS training. Alternatively, to locate a 3rd party certified lab near you to complete ASIN testing, please consult our APASS network list or leverage ISTAs Find a Lab search tool. For more information regarding fragile product definitions, see section below. Fragile material definition: Glass, Ceramic, Porcelain, Clay, Liquid, Semi-liquid, or Solids that liquefy at > Fahrenheit Fragile materials are products that could leak or break during the fulfillment process. If a product has % or greater of its total pieces containing fragile material, the product will be deemed fragile and  complete samples will be needed for testing. All others will only need one sample for certification. Example  piece pots/pans:  pots/pans,  glass lids- the sample would be deemed fragile (% of total  of  are fragile)

In summary, the two different testing requirements both enhance and protect customer experience. By permitting non-fragile products to be self-certified, we enable faster speed to market for this low damage risk category. For higher risk fragile and larger products over 50lbs, the full ISTA -Amazon.com-SIOC test method is required and protects brand and customer experience by preventing high damage rates. While the drop test seeks to identify serious problems that may arise from package handling, the full ISTA -Amazon.com-SIOC test allows a designer to foresee other shipment issues such as compression failures during storage, clamping and transport, abrasion due to vibration, and the cumulative effects of those distribution hazards. metadata.text: AMAZON CONFIDENTIAL Accessing Amazon Business How do I access my Amazon Business account? View step by step instructions on how to access your Amazon Business account in the User Registration Guide (PDF) that we have provided. If directed by my administrator, how do I deregister my account? . Log into your Business Account . Download an order history report for the past - months via Business Analytics from your drop-down menu . Click the following link to deregister your existing account: https://amazon.com/gp/b2b/manage/deregister NOTE: The terminology on this screen can be confusing. Rest assured your order history will not be lost, your account will be converted back to an Amazon.com consumer account, allowing you to join the new, central Amazon Business account. Then, follow the appropriate steps in the registration guide linked above to convert your account

metadata.text: Cancel Items and Orders You can cancel items or orders by visiting the Your Orders section in Your Account. To access in your preferred language, locate the country flag on top banner and use the drop down to select the language. To access in your preferred language, select 'You' icon at the bottom and select country flag available at top right corner to select the language. Want to cancel an item or order? Visit Your Orders To cancel orders before they are shipped: . Visit Your Orders. . Select the item you want to cancel and select Cancel items. . Provide reasons for cancellation (optional) and proceed. To cancel orders that are already shipped: . Visit Your Orders. . Select the Request Cancellation option and proceed. . The items will be returned to Amazon for a refund (if the payment is already made). metadata.text: . Potential root cause Resolution Pixel mapping is disabled Note: Stuck pixels are always completely bright, as if they're fully overexposed. Guidelines for keeping the Amazon community helpful, relevant, meaningful, and appropriate The community is a place to share your thoughts and experiences (positive and negative) with other customers. The following guidelines explain what's allowed and not allowed. By using community features, you agree to our Conditions of Use and Privacy Notice. You also agree to follow these guidelines, which we update from time to time. Community features include: Reviews (including star ratings) Questions and answers Helpful votes Lists and registries Profile pages Amazon Follow #FoundItOnAmazon Amazon digital and device forums Reports of violations What the guidelines apply to Actions you take, including: . Sharing text, photos, videos, links

metadata.text: Participate in digital and device forums To do any of the following, you need to have spent at least INR  on Amazon.in using any valid payment method for orders delivered in the past  months: Create reviews (including star ratings) Answer customer questions Submit helpful votes Create idea lists Follow other contributors Promotional discounts don't count towards the INR  minimum spending requirement. What's not allowed Seller, order, and shipping feedback We don't allow reviews or questions and answers that focus on: Sellers and the customer service they provide Ordering issues and returns Shipping and packaging Product condition and damage Shipping cost and speed Why not? Community content is meant to help customers learn about the product itself, not someone's individual experience ordering it. That said, we definitely want your feedback about sellers and packaging, just not in reviews or questions and answers
metadata.source: Community Guidelines - Amazon Customer Service.pdf

metadata.text: . That said, we definitely want your feedback about sellers and packaging, just not in reviews or questions and answers. Comments about pricing and availability It's OK to comment on price if it's related to the product's value. Example: "For only INR , this blender is really great." Pricing comments related to someone's individual experience aren't allowed. For example, don't compare the price of the same product at different stores: "Found this here for INR  less at my local store." These comments aren't allowed because they aren't relevant for all customers. //, : AM Community Guidelines - Amazon Customer Service https://www.amazon.in/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GLHXEX85MENUE4XF#GUID-EF335E34-AFE7--B8AA-8AD538A01C90__SECTI /

metadata.text: Some comments about availability are OK. For example, you can discuss formats that haven't been released yet: "I wish this book was also available in paperback." However, we don't allow comments about availability at a specific store. Again, the purpose of the community is to share product-specific feedback that's relevant to all other customers. Content written in unsupported languages To ensure that content is useful, we only allow it to be written in the supported languages of the Amazon site where it will appear. For example, we don't allow reviews written in French on Amazon.in, which only supports English, Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Malayalam, Bengali, and Marathi. Some Amazon sites support multiple languages, but content written in a mix of languages isn't allowed. Learn which languages are supported on this Amazon site. Repetitive text, spam, pictures created with symbols We don't allow contributions with distracting content and spam

id: Secure Amazon Delivery with 6 Digit Pin - Amazon Customer Service.pdf_p1_c190

metadata.text: Secure Delivery with a One-Time Password Learn more about Amazons six-digit delivery PIN and where you can find it. An OTP adds an extra layer of security for your order item(s). This six-digit OTP is different from the four-digit PIN that can be used to contact the delivery associate. To view in your preferred language, locate the country flag on the top banner and use the drop- down to select the language. Some high-valued 'Shipped by Amazon' item(s) and select 'Shipped by Amazon' item(s) will require a -digit OTP (One-time Password) to be provided at the time of delivery. What is Secure Delivery with OTP? Amazon delivery with OTP (One-time password) adds an extra layer of security to your packages. It is a six-digit number sent to your registered email address, when the package is Out for Delivery. It is valid until the end of the delivery day. Will I get OTP for delivery of all my orders? No, only selected products on Amazon.in will require an OTP for successful delivery
metadata.source: Secure Amazon Delivery with 6 Digit Pin - Amazon Customer Service.pdf

id: Secure Amazon Delivery with 6 Digit Pin - Amazon Customer Service.pdf_p1_c191

metadata.text: . Will I get OTP for delivery of all my orders? No, only selected products on Amazon.in will require an OTP for successful delivery. I didn't get the OTP, where can I find it? You will receive the OTP in your registered email address, SMS, or WhatsApp Messages. You can also find the OTP in tracking details of Your Order. If you are unable to locate the OTP at the time of delivery, request the delivery associate to resend it to your registered mobile number. Note: A six-digit OTP is different from the four-digit PIN provided in your order tracking, which is used to contact the delivery associate when the item(s) is still out for delivery. When shall I share the OTP with Delivery Associate? Share the OTP with delivery associate in person at the time of Delivery Only. Do not share the OTP over the phone. If you are not available to receive the package, you can share the OTP with someone you trust, so they can receive the package. We don't recommend sharing the OTP with an unknown person
metadata.source: Secure Amazon Delivery with 6 Digit Pin - Amazon Customer Service.pdf

metadata.text: Frequently Asked Questions about Warranty Know about warranty, including how to avail it for products purchased on Amazon. . Where can I find the warranty information for the product I am purchasing, before purchase? The warranty information can be found on the product detail page. To learn more about the warranty details, simply click on the "shield" icon. . What do I see when I select the shield icon and how do I interpret it? Star within the shield icon: This warranty icon enables you, as a customer, to claim the warranty through your Amazon account. Tick within the shield icon: This warranty icon means the warranty will be provided by the manufacturer. If you reach out to the manufacturing brand and provide details from your invoice, the service center will schedule a convenient service slot for you. If you do not see a shield icon that would imply the brand has not shared their warranty information with us. Please reach out to the brand directly.

What is Order Management Process - The Ultimate Guide Learn about how the order management process works, different types of order management system, and how an order management cycle can help your business. By Amazon Business Editorial Desk Published on October ,  Organizing, managing and fulfilling customer orders is a critical process for any business. Without an efficient order management process in place, businesses can quickly find themselves overwhelmed and struggling to keep up with customer demand. In this blog, we will discuss what is order management process, the benefits of implementing an effective process and explore some best practices for streamlining the process. So, if you are looking to improve your order management system, keep reading! What Is Order Management? Order management deals with the creation and fulfillment of customer orders
metadata.source: What is Order Management Process - An Ultimate Guide _ Amazon Business.pdf

id: What is Order Management Process - An Ultimate Guide _ Amazon Business.pdf_p1_c262

metadata.text: . The order management process typically begins when a customer submits an order and ends when the order is either fulfilled or canceled. What Is the Order Management Process? The entire process, from receiving the customers order to the fulfillment of it, is called the order management process. Creating and updating orders is the first task in customer order management. This involves creating a new order in the system and then updating the order as needed. Updating an order may involve adding or removing items, changing the shipping address, or changing the payment method. metadata.text: Issues with Guitar Find answers to common guitar-related questions, including string replacement, tuning, cleaning, and basic setup. . When should I change the strings? Strings will deteriorate over time, causing buzzing or inaccurate pitch. There is no set rule for how often you should change your strings. If you have strings that are worn, flattened, kinked, corroded, rusted or mucked up with sweat grease and grime, then it is time to change your strings. Bent, twisted, or damaged strings will not produce the appropriate quality sound and therefore, should not be used. Otherwise, strings should be changed when they are no longer giving you the tone, brightness and response that you desire. Replace all the strings as a set. The strings should be replaced one by one instead of removing all at once. Wind the string around the tuning machine post two or three times, making sure to wind from top to bottom. Wind about  cm of string for guitar and  cm for bass
metadata.source: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf

id: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf_p1_c321

metadata.text: . Wind about  cm of string for guitar and  cm for bass. Do not wind the string on top of itself. . How do I tune my guitar? When looking straight down at your guitar the open strings from the thickest to the thinnest are EADgbe; E being the thickest string and e the thinnest string. Use a tuner or tuning fork to tune up the sound of each open string to ensure accurate adjustments. Place your index finger on the fifth fret of the string. Now pick the next string and fretted six string in turn, gently adjusting the next string tuning peg until the two notes are in harmony. (Follow this instruction for all the strings except for the G string, instead of placing the finger on the fifth fret, you need to place the finger on the fourth fret.) If the pitch is too high, loosen the string to lower the pitch, and wind the string in small increments to tune it up
metadata.source: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf

id: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf_p1_c322

metadata.text: .) If the pitch is too high, loosen the string to lower the pitch, and wind the string in small increments to tune it up. If youve changed string gauges or are using your guitar with an alternative tuning, youll need to adjust the string length (intonation) to ensure that the correct pitch is sounded at all frets. After tuning your guitar accurately, hold the guitar in playing position and compare the pitch of each string pressed down at the 12th fret with the pitch of the harmonic played at the 12th fret. If the pitch of the fretted note at the 12th fret is lower than the harmonic at that fret, move the saddle of the tremolo/bridge forward to shorten the string. Conversely, if the pitch of the fretted note is higher than the pitch of the harmonic, move the saddle backward to lengthen the string. //, : AM Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service https://www.amazon.in/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=Ttx3NoOeDlf6SpTJvy /

metadata.text: . How do I clean my guitar? After playing, wipe sweat and oil off metal parts such as the underside of the strings, the frets, bridge saddles and nut. This will help to prevent rust. Dirt or dust that adheres to metal parts may adversely affect their function. Wipe off stubborn dirt with a soft cloth moistened with a small amount of oil. To clean the finished surface, do not use volatile or abrasive cleaning compounds; instead, gently wipe using a soft cloth with polish formulated specifically for musical instruments. . Left Vs. Right-handed Guitar - which is better? If the (thicker/bigger/wider) bass strings are on your left and the (thinner/smaller/narrower) treble strings are on your right, then you are looking at a Righthanded Guitar
metadata.source: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf

metadata.text: . If you are wondering if you should buy a lefty guitar or a right-handed one and just flip it (or vice-versa), you might want to have into consideration that flipping has its limitations: Switches and tone pots - Pickup selector switches and volume and tone knobs will likely be in a less convenient location. On right-handed guitars, these controls are placed for easy access with the right hand while playing. Unless they are moved and rewired, they actually may get in the way of your picking. Tremolo arms - Having the whammy bar on the top side of the guitar is a pain for lefties. The tremolo bridge could be customized to mount the bar below the strings in the lefty position, but its probably simpler to forego it if you are not using a lot of vibrato bends. Intonation - Restringing a right-hand guitar as a lefty can cause problems when trying to intonate each string during setup
metadata.source: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf

id: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf_p2_c325

metadata.text: . Intonation - Restringing a right-hand guitar as a lefty can cause problems when trying to intonate each string during setup. Setup of a flipped electric guitar is easier if it has adjustable bridge saddles that allow fine-tuning each strings height. The compensated bridges found on acoustic guitars will require modification to work as a lefty. Input jack - Often the jack is angled so the cable drapes directly toward the floor. When flipped the cable will emerge from the jack pointing toward you. Guitars with the jack built into the top of the guitar can also be problematic depending on the location. Pickups - Pickups with staggered pole pieces are designed specifically to work with each individual string, meaning that when the string order is reversed, the pickup does not work as intended. Typically, however, these can be replaced with a left-handed pickup. Pickguard  The pickguard (if your guitar has one) will be facing the wrong direction once the instrument is flipped
metadata.source: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf

id: Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service.pdf_p2_c326

metadata.text: . Pickguard  The pickguard (if your guitar has one) will be facing the wrong direction once the instrument is flipped. While this may not be important to you, it could lead to extra wear on the body of the guitar. Ultimately, most of the issues youll run into can be fixed with some modifications, but keep in mind that these will add to the purchase price. . How can I connect Amps and Pedals? Connect the amp's power cord to the wall. Plug your guitar cable into the guitars input jack. The guitar's jack should be on the front of the guitar or the edge of the guitar and will look like a metallic socket. //, : AM Issues with Guitar - Amazon Customer Service https://www.amazon.in/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=Ttx3NoOeDlf6SpTJvy /

metadata.text: Turn down the volume and the gain of your amp to prevent feedback and will stop you from blowing out your speaker when you plug in your guitar. Take the other end of your guitar's cable and plug it into the input jack on your amplifier. Turn your amp on and turn the volume and gain up until your guitar reaches a suitable volume. Test other knobs on the guitar like bass, mid, and treble knobs. Start these knobs off in the  o'clock position to give your guitar a default tone. Then, start to adjust the different knobs and play your guitar until it reaches the sound that you desire. You can also connect your guitar to a pedal using an instrument cable. Plug one end to your guitar and the other end into the input jack on your guitar pedal. You will need another instrument cable to plug one side into the output jack on your pedal and the other end into the input jack on the amp. You will need to repeat this process if you want to add more pedals

metadata.text: . Identifying the correct Browse Node ID The Browse Node ID is the product category in which the item is classified. Correct classification of your product in the respective Browse Node is of decisive significance for its visibility. Imagine the category structure as a tree with branches (subcategories) and leaves (Leaf Node). The branches represent the respective subcategory; the leaves are the final points within the structure where the customer cannot specify the category any further. *Please note that for Personal Care Appliance products Browse Nodes can be found in our Health and Personal Care Browse Tree Guide (BTG)
